title: SpaceX launching powerful Falcon Heavy rocket for 1st time in 18 months on April 27: Watch it live
summary: SpaceX is set to launch its Falcon Heavy rocket for the first time in 18 months on April 27, with the event available to watch live.

title: The 'Oscars of Science': Breakthrough Prize 2026 awards over $18 million for discoveries across space, physics and more
summary: The 2026 Breakthrough Prize awarded over $18 million to recognize groundbreaking discoveries in dark matter, quantum physics, gene editing, and nonlinear mathematics. The prestigious awards are often referred to as the 'Oscars of Science.'

title: The moon as you rarely see it: How a photographer captured night and day on the first quarter moon
summary: A photographer captured a rare image of the first quarter moon showing both night and day sides. The achievement was described as incredibly rewarding for accurately representing the moon's appearance.

title: 15 expert-checked places to see the 2026 total solar eclipse in Spain, Iceland and Greenland
summary: The article lists 15 prime locations in Spain, Iceland, and Greenland to view the total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, highlighting diverse scenic spots like Arctic fjords and volcanic craters.

title: Satellite snaps amazing 36th birthday pic of Hubble Space Telescope (photo)
summary: A private WorldView Legion 4 Earth-observing satellite captured a photo of the Hubble Space Telescope on April 23, 2026, one day before its 36th anniversary.

title: From Apollo to alien worlds: 4 'firsts' you can spot in the night sky tonight
summary: The article highlights four significant firsts in astronomy that can be observed in the night sky, spanning from the Apollo missions to the discovery of alien worlds.

title: Russia launches 3 tons of cargo to the International Space Station from Kazakhstan (video)
summary: Russia launched a Progress cargo spacecraft carrying 3 tons of supplies to the International Space Station from Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan on April 25. The mission marks another resupply effort to support the orbiting laboratory.

title: How did humanity end up underground in 'Silo'? Apple TV digs deep in new Season 3 teaser (video)
summary: Apple TV+ released a teaser for Season 3 of the sci-fi series 'Silo', exploring the origins of humanity's underground existence. The show's narrative centers on uncovering the mystery behind the underground society depicted in the series.

title: I Am Artemis: Peter Rossoni
summary: Peter Rossoni, Orion Artemis II Optical Communications System flight manager, reflects on his childhood inspiration from Apollo missions and his role in NASA's Artemis II mission, enabling communications for astronauts around the Moon in April 2026.

title: The Day of the Trifid Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a detailed visible-light image of the Trifid Nebula, 5,000 light-years from Earth, to celebrate the mission's 36th launch anniversary on April 24, 2026.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Missouri Students
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack Hathaway will answer STEM questions from Missouri students during a live Earth-to-space call on April 30. The event will be streamed on NASA's YouTube channel.

title: NASA Celebrates Decade of University Innovation in Aeronautics
summary: NASA's University Leadership Initiative (ULI) has celebrated a decade of fostering aeronautical innovations and training over 1,100 students across 100 schools, continuing to drive advancements in 21st-century air travel.

title: Johnson Leaders Honored by National Space Club & Foundation
summary: Two leaders from NASA's Johnson Space Center were honored by the National Space Club & Foundation for their contributions to human spaceflight. Howard Hu, Orion Program Manager, received the Norman L. Baker Astronautics Engineer Award for sustained technical contributions.

title: NASA Shares SpaceX Crew-13 Assignments for Space Station Mission
summary: NASA has announced the crew assignments for the SpaceX Crew-13 mission to the International Space Station, including astronauts from NASA and CSA. The mission is scheduled for mid-September and will focus on a long-duration science expedition.

title: NASA Kennedy Prepares Facility for Roman Space Telescope Arrival
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center is preparing facilities for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for early September aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket. The telescope aims to deliver unprecedented deep, panoramic views of the cosmos to advance our understanding of the universe.

title: NASA Welcomes Jordan as 63rd Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Jordan became the 63rd signatory to the Artemis Accords, committing to responsible space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Jordan's participation during a ceremony at NASA headquarters.

title: Liquid Lifeline: NASA Tech Could Create IV Fluid In Space
summary: NASA is developing technology to produce IV fluid in space, which could be crucial for crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit. This innovation could reduce reliance on pre-packed medical supplies and enhance astronaut safety.

title: SpaceX set to launch Falcon Heavy on Monday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX is preparing to launch its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle following its last mission in June 2019.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: The Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission two weeks prior.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on its Starship program at Cape Canaveral, with hopes to launch the Block 3 variant later this year. The first flight of Block 3 is approaching as the company advances its development.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raised concerns about the program's timeline and potential impacts on future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled across four countries using five different launch vehicles during the week of April 20. This highlights a busy period for global space activities.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the New Glenn launch. This follows Blue Origin's recent milestone of landing a reused first-stage booster.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This marks a significant milestone in the development of partially reusable orbital launch systems.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an Astronaut Flight Suit and a Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This marks a step toward commercial space station development.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests as part of the Artemis program. This marks a significant milestone for NASA's upcoming missions.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions to space exploration and science.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or spacecraft. This innovation could significantly enhance in-space servicing and debris removal operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has passed rigorous space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on 8–9 June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, with an online preparatory forum in April and May. The event aims to gather feedback on topics affecting the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received. This marks a significant milestone for the space industry.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improvements in attitude control systems. It highlights innovations that enhance precision in directional alignment for space missions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials with greater efficiency in space-like conditions.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon has selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI Tech initiative, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion in total. This move aims to enhance space-based capabilities for national security.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to reduce costs and scale operations for commercial and government customers. The initiative targets the 'Golden Dome' as part of their strategy.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Donald Trump has appointed two space industry executives to key military space roles, pending Senate confirmation. The nominees will require Senate approval before assuming their positions.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center. The engine could potentially power future Moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: A cost analysis compares the build costs of an orbital data center with a terrestrial one, including opportunity costs for terrestrial delays. The analysis was published by Payload Pro.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has secured €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have pledged to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, stating the budget proposal is insufficient to meet the agency's mandated goals. The statement comes in response to President Trump's directives for NASA.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships, suggesting that the Navy could assist in tracking these satellites. It highlights a potential mutual benefit between satellite tracking and naval operations.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has secured €25.7 million in Series A funding to transition to regular operational service. The company is developing commercial, institutional, and defense-capable space logistics solutions with platforms like ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, initially announced in January 2025, is a missile defense program aiming to protect the US, attracting significant interest from companies. The program is positioned as a major competition in the space industry.

title: Space Force Awards $3.2 Billion to 12 Companies for Space-Based Interceptor Work
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 12 companies contracts worth up to $3.2 billion to develop space-based interceptor prototypes for the Golden Dome initiative. This move aims to enhance missile defense capabilities in space.

title: GomSpace to Establish New Ukrainian Joint Venture for Sovereign Comms
summary: GomSpace, a Danish satellite company, is establishing a new joint venture in Ukraine to develop sovereign satellite communications capabilities. The partnership is with Ukrainian firm Stetman.

title: Rocket Lab Introduces Next-Generation Star Tracker Component
summary: Rocket Lab has launched its next-generation High-Performance Star Tracker (ST-HP) component, designed for extended spacecraft missions. This expansion strengthens the company's offerings in space navigation systems.

title: FCC Rejects Requests for Sharing in MSS Spectrum, Maintaining Exclusive Rights
summary: The FCC rejected requests from SpaceX and other operators to share MSS spectrum bands, maintaining exclusive licensing rights. The decision was formalized in an order issued on Thursday.

title: SDA’s Need for Speed Pushes Startups for Results
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is pausing launches until May to address technology hurdles, supply chain challenges, and balance speed with results. Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo emphasized the need for startups to deliver under these strategic delays.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to deploy compact teleports at Equinx's data centers to support its Meridian satellite constellation. This partnership aims to enhance ground infrastructure for SpinLaunch's space launch technology.

title: Iridium Continues IoT Subscriber Growth in Q1, Desch Talks NTN Direct Launch and Spectrum
summary: Iridium reported a 2% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 2026, driven by continued growth in IoT subscribers. CEO Matt Desch discussed plans for NTN direct launches and spectrum management.

title: Univity Raises Further Funding for VLEO Constellation for Europe
summary: Univity, a French constellation startup, raised a 27 million euro ($32 million) Series A round to fund two demonstration satellites for a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) connectivity constellation in Europe.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: U.S. officials criticize the proposed EU Space Act, viewing it as an overreach by the European Union. The act aims to create a unified regulatory framework for space activities.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Jetstar Airways, an Australian low-cost airline, has chosen Viasat's Amara solution for in-flight connectivity on its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed on 11 Jetstar aircraft.
